samples.xml_description:
Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers. Clock signal. Reset signal. Signed or unsigned operation. Input operand DIVIDEND. Input operand DIVISOR. Valid operation request. The output has been acknowledged. The result is valid. Output result. 0 1 2 3 4 5 Counter for the division process. Start control for the division process. Two's complement of the divisor. Shift register for the division process.

When

opn_valid is high and res_valid is low,

the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.

Initializes the shift register sr with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.

Initializes neg_divisor to the negated absolute value of the divisor.

Initializes the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.

When start_cnt is high,

  1. If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:

    • cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
    • Updates the shift register sr with the final remainder and quotient.

  2. Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented,

    and the shift register sr is updated based on the subtraction result:

    • sub_result is computed as the subtraction of neg_divisor.
    • mux_result is selected based on the carry-out.
    • sr is shifted left and updated with the carry-out.

When res_valid is high and res_ready is low,

res_valid is cleared to low.

Otherwise, if res_valid is high and res_ready is high,

res_valid is set to low.
